name: Beijer Electronics Group AB (publ)
summary: Beijer Electronics Group AB (publ), together with its subsidiaries, provides digital industrial Internet of things (IIoT) solutions integrating services, software, and hardware in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Germany, the United Kingdom, France, Turkey, rest of Europe, the United States, China, Taiwan, rest of Asia, and internationally. It operates through Westermo, Beijer Electronics, and Korenix segments. It offers control systems; frequency inverters; operator panels and human machine interface solutions; protocol converters, IIoT gateways, and edge controllers; servo drives and motors, and motion controllers; and network adapter modules, digital input and output modules, analog input and output modules, and special and power modules, as well as a range of distributed IOs for hot swap and advanced functions. The company also provides Ethernet switches, PoE switches, 3G/4G routers, wireless products, media converters and PoE injectors, serial and USB converters, and remote access products, as well as network management software; and industrial power supplies. In addition, it offers automation software under the iX HMI, CODESYS, WARP, and BFI Tools names. The company markets its products to the energy, water, marine and offshore, infrastructure, buildings, transportation, food and beverage, packaging, aquaculture and fish farming, and manufacturing sectors under the Beijer Electronics, Westermo, and Korenix brand names. Beijer Electronics Group AB (publ) sells its products through system integrators, original equipment manufacturers, brand label customers, and distributors. The company was founded in 1981 and is headquartered in Malm√É¬∂, Sweden.
